videos.title: 2012 RBC GranFondo Banff - Official Video
videos.description:
http://banff.granfondocanada.com/
The inaugural RBC GranFondo Banff took place on August 25, 2012. Riders were guided through a breathtaking course fully contained within Canada's oldest national park, Banff National Park.

Like us on Facebook at: http://www.facebook.com/GranFondoCanadaBanff

videos.title: 2012 RBC GranFondo Banff - Course Preview
videos.description:
http://www.banff.granfondocanada.com/
The inaugural RBC GranFondo Banff took place on August 25, 2012. Let us guide you through the breathtaking 142 km course. Riders rode the only gran fondo in North America that is fully contained within the boundaries of a national park.

Visit us at www.banff.granfondocanada.com/

Like us on Facebook at: http://www.facebook.com/GranFondoCanadaBanff
videos.transcript:
Grand Fondo Canada is pleased to Welcome
All Riders to the 2012 RBC Grand Fondo
band our goal is to provide you with an
incredibly beautiful and memorable
cycling experience all here in B
National Park Canada's oldest National
Park the start of the 142 km ride begins
at the corner of Buffalo and bear
streets at Central Park in downtown B
please ride your bike to the start and
leave your car at your hotel or visit
August 25th. ca for places to park we
will start in waves and a neutral roll
out for the first 10 km with maximum
speeds controlled and limited to 30
kmph the ride starts off with a climb
from Central Park up Buffalo Street and
along Tunnel Mountain
Drive off to the right you'll have
excellent views of the iconic 100 24y
old bamp Springs
hotel where Tunnel Mountain Drive meets
Tunnel Mountain Road we take a right
turn be cautious here as you round the
corner the hudo Viewpoint offers a
spectacular view of Mount Rundle and the
bo River we're not far from the
headwaters of the bo River and these
Waters will eventually find their way to
Hudson Bay 1,500 km from here
the neutralized start will end where you
leave Tunnel Mountain Road from here the
pack will stretch out as Riders find
their own
pace Wildlife Gates will be covered but
please use caution we'll be Crossing
five of them on the
course we're now heading out on the lake
minow wanka loop it's a spectacular
circuit and a favorite with local
Riders this is one of parks Canada's
busiest campgrounds located on the
picturesque two Jack
Lake it's also a popular area with
wildlife and mountain sheep are
frequently seen in this
area lake minona is one of the most

the lake is man-made to supply power to
the town of remains of an Old Town site
that was flooded when the lake was
created still exist
underwater the Northern Shores of Lake

cougars to of Vamps wild
inhabitants the first rest off is
approximately 20 km into the ride and
located under the Towering slopes of
Cascade
Mountain there will be a 10 km stretch
along the Trans Canada Highway a buffer
Lane for emergency vehicles will be
maintained but cyclists must not cross
the white line all Riders must ride on
the shoulder until we get to the
Beautiful b Valley Parkway
Wildlife gates are used to keep animals
off the highway animals are scared to
walk across the Gated surface and so are
many
humans the sawback prescribed burn area
provides great views and also acts as a
fire break for band and a regeneration
opportunity for the
forest rest stop number two is at
Johnston's Canyon this spectacular
Johnston's Canyon is a very popular
hiking and sightseeing destination with
beautiful Chalet and a campground
nearby the broad B Valley contains the
main line for the Canadian Pacific
Railway originally built between 1881
and 1885 these tracks connect the
Atlantic and the Pacific Ocean and we're
a major driver of Tourism to western
Canada in the late
1800s we're crossing those tracks once
today but don't worry if you're stopped
by a train we'll deduct the time you
waited
the turnaround point is at the crest of
the hill before white Horn Road to Lake
Louise this is also restof number three
a great opportunity to refresh yourself
while enjoying views of the spectacular
peaks in the Lake Louise area you're
more than halfway now and on your way
back to
B wave to the fans guests and Spectators
who are staying at the baker Creek
Chalet or cheering you on
rest stop number four is at Castle
Mountain Chalet take in some
refreshments and extra special treats
for the final push to the Finish Line be
sure to take in the breathtaking view of
Castle Mountain before you get back on
your
bike as you rejoin the Trans Canada
Highway you're only 7 km from the Finish
don't forget to stay on the
shoulder Vermillion lakes in the
surrounding Wetland stretch to the town
of B
the Viewpoint here offers great views of
sulfur and Rundle
[Music]
mountains coming back into the bamp town
site you're less than 2 km from the
finish right back where you
started after 142 km it's time to
celebrate with your fellow
Riders you have signed up for a
spectacular event we know you're going
to enjoy it and look forward to seeing
you in b

videos.title: Granfondo Banff 2016
videos.description: www.deporteshoy.ca // @rogersounders  // The Granfondo Banff, one of the greatest bike rides in the country, the best organized and the cleanest in the Banff National Park. - To make clarifications, Youtube has been blocking our videos because according to them, we are doing copyrights because of the background music when starting the bike rides. We want to clarify that it is ridiculous because we do not copyright songs, we do not need to make money, this is only a video that allows viewers to enjoy cycling, promoting the bike race for new participants and with the purpose of entertaining our viewers. No copyright material is our own interest and we cannot ask Granfondo Canada to turn off the music when riders start their journey. -  Thank you.

videos.title: Gran Fondo Banff 2015
videos.description:
Gran Fondo Banff 2015, 150km of pedaling within the Banff National Park with +1000 cyclists on Aug 22, 2015. We started from the Central Park in the Town of Banff, climbed up Tunnel Mountain right from the start, went back down, left town to do a loop by Two Jack Lake and Lake Minnewanka. We came back to the Town of Banff and climbed Tunnel Mountain again. Then we left town and went to the turnaround at Lake Louise on the Bow Valley Parkway. New this year, there was an official timed Strava KOM/QOM segment. The weather was a bit cold again, 4-5C brrrrr! It was a fun and challenging ride. Cya next year!

List of background music.

It's All Happening by Huma-Huma

Not For Nothing by Otis McDonald

 Go Cart (Loop Mix) by Kevin MacLeod is licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution license (https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by/4.0/)
Source: http://incompetech.com/music/royalty-free/index.html?isrc=USUAN1300008
Artist: http://incompetech.com/ 

Friction Looks by Silent Partner

Drop It by Silent Partner

Never Better by Jingle Punks

videos.title: Simon Whitfield Riding in the Inaugural RBC GranFondo Banff
videos.description:
http://banff.granfondocanada.com/
Count yourself in. Ride shoulder to shoulder with Olympic Gold Medalist and proud Canadian Simon Whitfield in the inaugural RBC GranFondo Banff on August 25, 2012.

Registration opens on March 15, 2012. For more information, visit: http://banff.granfondocanada.com/

Visit us on Facebook at: http://www.facebook.com/granfondocanadabanff
